title: eriogonum covilleanum subsp. adsurgens
intro: Eriogonum covilleanum subsp. adsurgens, also known as Coville's buckwheat, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the buckwheat manzanita plant, native to the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Eriogonum covilleanum subsp. adsurgens include its potential to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, making it a promising natural remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
